audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar second-census and inheritance text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for post-plague census, tribal clan totals, Korah survival note, daughters of Zelophehad, land-by-lot inheritance, Levite census, and generational transition. Anti-militarization, gender-justice, and anti-human-worth safeguards required.

verses.restored: After the plague, YHWH spoke to Moses and to Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, saying,
Lift up the head/count all congregation of children of Israel from twenty years old and upward, by houses of their fathers, everyone going out to host in Israel.
Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke with them in plains of Moab by Jordan at Jericho, saying,
From twenty years old and upward, as YHWH commanded Moses and children of Israel who came out from land of Egypt.
Reuben, firstborn of Israel: sons of Reuben were Hanoch, clan of Hanochites; of Pallu, clan of Palluites;
of Hezron, clan of Hezronites; of Carmi, clan of Carmites.
These were clans of Reubenites; those counted of them were forty-three thousand seven hundred thirty.
Son of Pallu was Eliab.

Sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram. These Dathan and Abiram were summoned ones of congregation who contended against Moses and Aaron in congregation of Korah, when they contended against YHWH.
Earth opened its mouth and swallowed them together with Korah when congregation died, when fire consumed two hundred fifty men; and they became a sign.
But sons of Korah did not die.
Sons of Simeon by clans: of Nemuel, clan of Nemuelites; of Jamin, clan of Jaminites; of Jachin, clan of Jachinites;
of Zerah, clan of Zerahites; of Shaul, clan of Shaulites.
These were clans of Simeonites: twenty-two thousand two hundred.
Sons of Gad by clans: of Zephon, clan of Zephonites; of Haggi, clan of Haggites; of Shuni, clan of Shunites;
of Ozni, clan of Oznites; of Eri, clan of Erites;
of Arod, clan of Arodites; of Areli, clan of Arelites.
These were clans of sons of Gad according to those counted of them: forty thousand five hundred.
Sons of Judah were Er and Onan, and Er and Onan died in land of Canaan.
Sons of Judah by clans were: of Shelah, clan of Shelanites; of Perez, clan of Perezites; of Zerah, clan of Zerahites.
Sons of Perez were: of Hezron, clan of Hezronites; of Hamul, clan of Hamulites.
These were clans of Judah according to those counted of them: seventy-six thousand five hundred.
Sons of Issachar by clans: Tola, clan of Tolaites; of Puvah, clan of Punites;
of Jashub, clan of Jashubites; of Shimron, clan of Shimronites.
These were clans of Issachar according to those counted of them: sixty-four thousand three hundred.
Sons of Zebulun by clans: of Sered, clan of Seredites; of Elon, clan of Elonites; of Jahleel, clan of Jahleelites.
These were clans of Zebulunites according to those counted of them: sixty thousand five hundred.
Sons of Joseph by clans were Manasseh and Ephraim.
Sons of Manasseh: of Machir, clan of Machirites; Machir fathered Gilead; of Gilead, clan of Gileadites.
These were sons of Gilead: of Iezer, clan of Iezerites; of Helek, clan of Helekites;
of Asriel, clan of Asrielites; of Shechem, clan of Shechemites;
of Shemida, clan of Shemidaites; of Hepher, clan of Hepherites.
Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons, but daughters; names of daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
These were clans of Manasseh; those counted of them were fifty-two thousand seven hundred.
These were sons of Ephraim by clans: of Shuthelah, clan of Shuthelahites; of Becher, clan of Becherites; of Tahan, clan of Tahanites.
These were sons of Shuthelah: of Eran, clan of Eranites.
These were clans of sons of Ephraim according to those counted of them: thirty-two thousand five hundred. These were sons of Joseph by clans.
Sons of Benjamin by clans: of Bela, clan of Belaites; of Ashbel, clan of Ashbelites; of Ahiram, clan of Ahiramites;
of Shupham, clan of Shuphamites; of Hupham, clan of Huphamites.
Sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: of Ard, clan of Ardites; of Naaman, clan of Naamites.
These were sons of Benjamin by clans; those counted of them were forty-five thousand six hundred.
These were sons of Dan by clans: of Shuham, clan of Shuhamites. These were clans of Dan by clans.
All clans of Shuhamites according to those counted of them were sixty-four thousand four hundred.
Sons of Asher by clans: of Imnah, clan of Imnites; of Ishvi, clan of Ishvites; of Beriah, clan of Beriites.
Of sons of Beriah: of Heber, clan of Heberites; of Malchiel, clan of Malchielites.
Name of daughter of Asher was Serah.
These were clans of sons of Asher according to those counted of them: fifty-three thousand four hundred.
Sons of Naphtali by clans: of Jahzeel, clan of Jahzeelites; of Guni, clan of Gunites;
of Jezer, clan of Jezerites; of Shillem, clan of Shillemites.
These were clans of Naphtali by clans; those counted of them were forty-five thousand four hundred.
These were those counted of children of Israel: six hundred one thousand seven hundred thirty.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
To these the land shall be divided as inheritance according to number of names.
To the many you shall increase inheritance, and to the few you shall diminish inheritance; each shall be given inheritance according to those counted of it.
Yet by lot land shall be divided; according to names of tribes of their fathers they shall inherit.
According to mouth of lot its inheritance shall be divided between many and few.
These were those counted of Levites by clans: of Gershon, clan of Gershonites; of Kohath, clan of Kohathites; of Merari, clan of Merarites.
These were clans of Levi: clan of Libnites, clan of Hebronites, clan of Mahlites, clan of Mushites, clan of Korahites. Kohath fathered Amram.
Name of Amram's wife was Jochebed daughter of Levi, whom she bore to Levi in Egypt; she bore to Amram Aaron and Moses and Miriam their sister.
To Aaron were born Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
Nadab and Abihu died when they brought near unauthorized fire before YHWH.

Those counted of Levites were twenty-three thousand, every male from one month old and upward; for they were not counted among children of Israel, because no inheritance was given to them among children of Israel.
These were those counted by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who counted children of Israel in plains of Moab by Jordan at Jericho.
Among these there was not a man from those counted by Moses and Aaron the priest, who counted children of Israel in wilderness of Sinai.
For YHWH had said of them, They shall surely die in wilderness. Not one of them remained except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.

translation_notes: The second census marks generational transition after the wilderness deaths; it is not a ranking of human worth.
Military-age language remains descriptive of ancient camp organization and must not define belonging, masculinity, disability, or value.
Numbers 26:11 explicitly states that sons of Korah did not die, qualifying broad household language in Numbers 16.
The daughters of Zelophehad are named within the census before their legal petition in chapter 27, preserving women's presence in the inheritance story.
Land allocation combines proportional population size with lot, balancing need and tribal inheritance tradition.
Levites are counted separately because their inheritance structure differs, not because they are superior.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 26 to value people by military capacity, male lineage, fertility, tribe, or census inclusion. The chapter records a changing generation and prepares an inheritance system that will soon be challenged and expanded by named women.
